This document will walk you through setting up, using, and customizing the theme provider for your React Native application.
`ThemeProvider` is a powerful, context-based theming solution that supports dynamic theme switching, light/dark mode, and custom color extensions.


## Introduction

`ThemeProvider` is a React Context Provider that enables you to define a global theme for your application or specific parts of it, built on React's Context API.
By wrapping your components with `ThemeProvider`, you can ensure that all nested components have access to a consistent set of styling variables, such as colors, typography, spacing, and more.
This promotes design consistency and simplifies theme management across your application.

**Key Features:**

*   **Centralized Theming:** Provide a consistent theme across all components.
*   **Dynamic Switching:** Change themes or color schemes on the fly.
*   **Persistence:** Abstract methods (`onLoad`, `onChangeTheme`, etc.) to easily integrate with storage solutions like `AsyncStorage`.
*   **typescript showLineNumbers Support:** Strongly typed for better developer experience and safety.
*   **Extensible:** Easily add your own custom colors to the theme.
*   **Easy to Consume:** Simple hooks (`useThemeState`, `useThemeColors`) make it easy to access theme values.

## Installation

Before using `ThemeProvider`, ensure you have installed the necessary dependencies.
Typically, this context is part of a larger UI library, such as `@flexnative/theme-context`.
You can install it using your preferred package manager:


```bash
npm install @flexnative/theme-context
# or
yarn add @flexnative/theme-context
```

## API

```bash
import { ThemeProvider } from '@flexnative/theme-context';
```

### Component
**`ThemeProvider<TColors>`**
This is the abstract class you must extend to create your own theme provider.

Type: 
```typescript
export default abstract class ThemeProvider<TColors> extends React.PureComponent<ThemeProviderProps<TColors>, StateProps<TColors>>
```

### Generics
*   `TColors` (optional): An object type to extend the theme's `colors` property.

### `StateProps<TColors, TState = {}>`
Defines the state properties for the ThemeProvider component.
This type combines the base theme state with any additional custom state properties defined by the consumer of the `ThemeProvider`.

|  Name   | Type | Required | Default Value | Description |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| theme | [`BaseTheme<TColors>`](#basethemetcolors) | true | [Default Theme](./default-theme.mdx) | The current theme object. It is optional to allow for asynchronous loading. |
| TColors | `object` | false |  | `template` The type for the custom colors in the theme. |
| TState | `object` | false |  | `template` An optional type for additional state properties, defaulting to an empty object. |

### Properties

|  Name   | Type | Required | Default Value | Description |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| theme | [`BaseTheme<TColors>`](#basethemetcolors) | true | [Default Theme](./default-theme.mdx) | Defines the structure of a theme object. |
| children | `React.ReactNode` | true |  | Your application's components. |

#### BaseTheme\<TColors\>
Defines the structure of a theme object.

|  Name   | Type | Required | Default Value | Description |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| colors | [`BaseColors & TColors`](./theme-provider.mdx#basecolors) | true | [Default Colors](./default-theme#colors) | Default theme colors. You can extend the `BaseColors` with your own color properties. |
| scheme | [`ColorSchemeName`](https://reactnative.dev/docs/usecolorscheme#supported-color-schemes) | false | Device color scheme. | The current color scheme, either 'light' or 'dark'. This is used to determine the overall theme style. |
| isDark | `boolean` | false | `false` | Readonly indicates whether the theme is in dark mode. |
| spaces | <code>Record&lt;<a href="#spaces">Spaces</a>, number&gt;</code> | false | [Spaces](./default-theme.mdx#spaces) | Spacing values for layout, such as margins and paddings. This is a key part of the theme that helps maintain consistent spacing across components. |
| fontSize | <code>Record&lt;<a href="#fontsize">FontSize</a>, number&gt;</code> | false | [FontSize](./default-theme.mdx#fontsize) | Font sizes for text elements. This allows you to define a consistent typography scale across your application. |
| borders | [`Borders`](#borders) | false | [`Borders`](./default-theme.mdx#borders) | Border styles for components. This can include border widths, radius to ensure a consistent look and feel across your UI components. | 

#### BaseColors
Defines the default color palette used in the theme.

|  Name   | Type | Required | Default Value | Description |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| white | `ColorValue` | true | | The default white color. |
| black | `ColorValue` | true | | The default black color. |
| primary | `ColorValue` | true | `| The primary color used for key UI elements. |
| primaryHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the primary color, used for buttons and interactive elements. |
| primaryActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the primary color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| primarySubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the primary color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| primarySubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the primary subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| primarySubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the primary subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| info | `ColorValue` | true | | The color used for informational messages and elements. |
| infoHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for informational elements. |
| infoActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for informational elements. |
| infoSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the info color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| infoSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the info subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| infoSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the info subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| success | `ColorValue` | true | | The color used for success messages and indicators. |
| successHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for success elements. |
| successActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for success elements. |
| successSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the success color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| successSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the success subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| successSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the success subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| warning | `ColorValue` | true | | The color used for warning messages and indicators. |
| warningHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for warning elements. |
| warningActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for warning elements. |
| warningSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the warning color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| warningSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the warning subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| warningSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the warning subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| error | `ColorValue` | true | | The color used for error messages and alerts. |
| errorHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for error elements. |
| errorActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for error elements. |
| errorSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the error color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| errorSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the error subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| errorSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the error subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| dark | `ColorValue` | true | | The dark color used for backgrounds and surfaces. |
| darkHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for dark color. |
| darkActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for dark color. |
| darkSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the dark color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| darkSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the dark subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| darkSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the dark subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states in dark color. |
| secondary | `ColorValue` | true | | The secondary color used for accents and highlights. |
| secondaryHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the secondary color, used for buttons and interactive elements. |
| secondaryActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the secondary color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| secondarySubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the secondary color, used for pressed buttons and active states.|
| secondarySubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the secondary subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| secondarySubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the secondary subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| light | `ColorValue` | true | | The light color used for backgrounds and surfaces. |
| lightHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for light color. |
| lightActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for light color. |
| lightSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the light color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| lightSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the light subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| lightSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the light subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| background | `ColorValue` | true | | The default background color for screens and components. |
| card | `ColorValue` | true | | The default color for card backgrounds. |
| default | `ColorValue` | true | | The default color used for text and icons. |
| defaultHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for default elements. |
| defaultActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for default elements. |
| defaultSubtle | `ColorValue` | true | | A subtle variant of the default color, used for backgrounds or less prominent elements. |
| defaultSubtleHover | `ColorValue` | true | | The hover state color for the default subtle color, used for interactive elements. |
| defaultSubtleActive | `ColorValue` | true | | The active state color for the default subtle color, used for pressed buttons and active states. |
| text | `ColorValue` | true | | The default text color for readability. |
| border | `ColorValue` | true | | The default border color for components. |
| placeholder | `ColorValue` | true | | The color used for placeholder text in inputs and other components. |

#### Spaces
Represents the possible spacing values used for margins, padding, etc.

***One of:***
 * `none` - No spacing.
 * `xxxs` - Spacing of 2 units.
 * `xxs` - Spacing of 4 units.
 * `xs` - Spacing of 6 units.
 * `sm` - Spacing of 8 units.
 * `md` - Spacing of 12 units.
 * `default` - Spacing of 16 units.
 * `lg` - Spacing of 18 units.
 * `xl` - Spacing of 24 units.
 * `xxl` - Spacing of 32 units.
 * `xxxl` - Spacing of 36 units.
 * `number` - A custom numeric spacing value.

 #### FontSize
Represents the possible font sizes used for text elements.

***One of:***
  - `xxs`: A small font size, 8 units.
  - `xs`: The default font size, 12 units.
  - `sm`: A medium font size, 14 units.
  - `md`: A large font size, 16 units.
  - `lg`: A large font size, 18 units.
  - `xl`: A large font size, 20 units.
  - `xxl`: A large font size, 24 units.
  - `number`: A custom numeric font size.

#### Borders
Defines the border styles used in the theme.

|  Name   | Type | Required | Default Value | Description |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| width | <code>Record&lt;<a href="#borderwidth">BorderWidth</a>, number&gt;</code> | false | [BorderWidth](./default-theme.mdx#borderwidth) | Border width values, where the keys are from the [`BorderWidth`](#borderwidth) type. |
| radius | <code>Record&lt;<a href="#borderradius">BorderRadius</a>, number&gt;</code> | false | [BorderRadius](./default-theme.mdx#borderradius) | Border radius values, where the keys are from the [`BorderRadius`](#borderradius) type. |

#### BorderWidth
Represents the possible border width values.

***One of:***
 * `none` - No border width (0).
 * `hairline` - A hairline border width, typically the thinnest possible line on the device. See [StyleSheet.hairlineWidth](https://reactnative.dev/docs/stylesheet#hairlinewidth). 
 * `thin` - A thin border width, 1 unit.
 * `base` - A base border width, 2 unit2.
 * `thick` - A thick border width, 3 unit.
 * `number` - A custom numeric border width.

#### BorderRadius
Represents the possible border radius values.

***One of:***
 - `none`: No border radius (0).
 - `small`: A small border radius (2).
 - `medium`: A medium border radius (4).
 - `large`: A large border radius (6).
 - `full`: A fully rounded border radius (99999).
 - `number`: A custom numeric border radius.

### Abstract Methods to Implement

You must provide an implementation for these methods in your subclass.

*  `onLoad(): Promise<void>`:
    Called on `componentDidMount`. Use this to load a saved theme from persistent storage.

*   `onChangeTheme<T>(arg: T): Promise<void>`:
     This method can be overridden to handle changes in the theme.
     It enables you to update the theme object and persist the user's chosen theme.

*   `onChangeColorScheme(colorScheme: ColorSchemeName): Promise<void>`:
    This abstract method should be implemented to handle changes in the color scheme
    (e.g., 'light' or 'dark'). It allows you to update the color scheme and persist the user's preference.

## Getting Started
Follow these three steps to integrate `ThemeProvider` into your application.

### Step 1: Create Your Custom ThemeProvider

Since `ThemeProvider` is an abstract class, you need to create your own implementation.
See the example on [how to implement your own ThemeProvider](./examples).
This is where you'll define how themes are loaded and saved. For this example, we'll use `AsyncStorage`.

First, ensure you have `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` installed.

```typescript showLineNumbers
// src/theme/MyThemeProvider.tsx
import { ColorSchemeName } from 'react-native';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import { ThemeProvider, BaseTheme, BaseColors, defaultTheme } from '@flexnative/theme-context';

// This is our concrete implementation of the abstract ThemeProvider
export class MyThemeProvider extends ThemeProvider<{}> {

  // Load theme from AsyncStorage when the app starts
  async onLoad(): Promise<void> {
    try {
      const savedTheme = await AsyncStorage.getItem('app-theme');
      if (savedTheme) {
        this.setState({ theme: JSON.parse(savedTheme) });
        console.log('Theme loaded from storage.');
      } else {
        // If no theme is saved, use the default
        this.setState({ theme: defaultTheme() });
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to load theme:', error);
      // Fallback to default theme on error
      this.setState({ theme: defaultTheme() });
    }
  }

  // Save the new color scheme and update the theme
  async onChangeColorScheme(colorScheme: ColorSchemeName): Promise<void> {
    if (this.state.theme) {
      const newTheme = { ...this.state.theme, colorScheme };
      this.setState({ theme: newTheme });
      await this.onChangeTheme(newTheme);
      console.log('ColorScheme changed and saved:', colorScheme);
    }
  }

  // Save the entire theme object to AsyncStorage
  async onChangeTheme(theme: BaseTheme<{}>): Promise<void> {
    try {
      this.setState({ theme });
      await AsyncStorage.setItem('app-theme', JSON.stringify(theme));
      console.log('Theme saved to storage.');
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to save theme:', error);
    }
  }

  // Update only the colors of the current theme
  async setColors(colors: BaseColors & {}): Promise<void> {
    if (this.state.theme) {
      const newTheme: BaseTheme<{}> = { ...this.state.theme, colors };
      // `onChangeTheme` handles both state update and persistence
      await this.onChangeTheme(newTheme);
      console.log('Theme colors updated and saved.');
    }
  }
}
```

### Step 2: Wrap Your App
Wrap your root component (e.g., `App.tsx`) with the `MyThemeProvider` you just created. This makes the theme context available to all child components.

The power of ThemeProvider lies in its ability to accept an object `BaseTheme<TColors>` as a theme.
You can define a comprehensive theme object that includes colors, spacing, component-specific styles, or any other design tokens you need.

```typescript showLineNumbers
// App.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { MyThemeProvider } from './src/theme/MyThemeProvider';
import { MainScreen } from './src/screens/MainScreen';

const App = () => {
  return (
    <MyThemeProvider>
      <MainScreen />
    </MyThemeProvider>
  );
};

export default App;
```

### Step 3: Use the Theme in Components
Now you can access the theme's colors and properties in any component using the provided hooks.

#### Hooks
Use these hooks in your functional components to consume the theme context.

##### `useThemeContext<TColors>()`
Returns the full context object, giving you access to both the state and the methods to change it.
*   **Returns**: `ThemeContextProps<TColors>` which is `{ state, setTheme, setColors, setColorScheme }`.

```typescript showLineNumbers
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
// highlight-next-line
import { useThemeContext } from '@flexnative/theme-context';
  
const MyComponent = () => {
  const { state, setTheme, setColors, setColorScheme } = useThemeContext<MyColors>();
  // Use the theme data and functions here
  return (
    <View>
      <Text>Current theme name: {state.name}</Text>
    </View>
  );
};
```

##### `useThemeState<TColors>()`
A convenience hook that returns just the `state` object from the theme context.
*   **Returns**: `BaseTheme<TColors>` - The complete theme object (`{ name, colorScheme, colors, ... }`).

```typescript showLineNumbers
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
// highlight-next-line
import { useThemeState } from '@flexnative/theme-context';
 
const MyComponent = () => {
  const themeState = useThemeState<MyColors>();
  // Use the theme state here (e.g., themeState.colors.primary)
  return (
    <View>
      <Text>Current Color Primary: {themeState.colors.primary}</Text>
    </View>
  );
};
```

##### `useThemeColors<TColors>()`
A convenience hook that returns just the `colors` object from the theme state. This is the most commonly used hook for styling.
*   **Returns**: `BaseColors & TColors` - An object containing all theme colors.

```typescript showLineNumbers
// src/screens/MainScreen.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet, Button } from 'react-native';
import { useThemeColors, useThemeContext } from '@flexnative/theme-context';

export const MainScreen = () => {
  const colors = useThemeColors();
  const { setColorScheme, state } = useThemeContext();

  const toggleScheme = () => {
    const newScheme = state.colorScheme === 'light' ? 'dark' : 'light';
    setColorScheme(newScheme);
  };

  return (
    <View style={[styles.container, { backgroundColor: colors.background }]}>
      <Text style={[styles.text, { color: colors.text }]}>
        Current Color Scheme: {state.colorScheme}
      </Text>
      <Button title="Toggle Light/Dark Mode" onPress={toggleScheme} />
    </View>
  );
};

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex: 1,
    justifyContent: 'center',
    alignItems: 'center',
  },
  text: {
    fontSize: 20,
    marginBottom: 20,
  },
});
```



## Advanced Usage

The ThemeProvider is highly flexible and does not enforce a specific theme structure.
You can define your theme object in any way that makes sense for your application.
However, a common practice is to categorize design tokens logically.

### Extending with Custom Colors

You can easily add your own colors to the theme.

1.  **Define your custom color interface.**

```typescript showLineNumbers
// src/theme/types.ts
import { ColorValue } from 'react-native';

export interface MyCustomColors {
  brandPrimary: ColorValue;
  success: ColorValue;
}
```

#### 2. Use the generic in your `ThemeProvider` implementation.

```typescript showLineNumbers
// src/theme/MyThemeProvider.tsx
import { MyCustomColors } from './types';

// Pass the custom type as a generic
export class MyThemeProvider extends ThemeProvider<MyCustomColors> {
  // ... implement abstract methods, using BaseTheme<MyCustomColors>
}
```

#### 3. Access the custom colors in a component.

```typescript showLineNumbers
// src/components/MyButton.tsx
import { useThemeColors } from '@flexnative/theme-context';
import { MyCustomColors } from '../theme/types';

const MyButton = () => {
  // Pass the custom type to the hook
  const colors = useThemeColors<MyCustomColors>();

  return (
    <TouchableOpacity style={{ backgroundColor: colors.brandPrimary }}>
      <Text style={{ color: colors.white }}>Press Me</Text>
    </TouchableOpacity>
  );
};
```

### Switching Themes

You can create multiple theme objects and switch between them using the `setTheme` function from the context.

```typescript showLineNumbers showLineNumbers
// In some component...
import { useThemeContext, createTheme } from '@flexnative/theme-context';

const ThemeSwitcher = () => {
  const { setTheme, state } = useThemeContext();

  // Assume `darkTheme` and `lightTheme` are fully defined BaseTheme objects
  const darkTheme = createTheme({
    // ... other properties
  });

  const lightTheme = createTheme({
    // ... other properties
  });

  const applyDarkTheme = () => {
    setTheme(darkTheme);
  };

  const applyLightTheme = () => {
    setTheme(lightTheme);
  };

  return (
    <View>
      <Button title="Apply Dark Theme" onPress={applyDarkTheme} disabled={state.name === 'dark'}/>
      <Button title="Apply Light Theme" onPress={applyLightTheme} disabled={state.name === 'light'}/>
    </View>
  );
}
```
